title: cirsium lomatolepis
intro: Cirsium lomatolepis, also known as the woolly thistle, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids in its leaves and flowers. The medicinal properties of Cirsium lomatolepis have been reported to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, are typically prepared by infusing the dried leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent to extract its medicinal compounds.
